thoughts.subject: Chen Zian
thoughts.description: Chen Zian An Lushan's offer to make inquiries among his contacts in Chang'an about the examiners' preferences, and his mention that wealthy households employ tutors and have connections to officials who may know the examiners' preferences,

thoughts.subject: Chen Zian
thoughts.description: This is Chen Zian's plan for Sunday February 15: wake up and complete the morning routine at 7:00 am, review Confucian classics at the inn from 7:30 to 10:00 am, have a simple breakfast at 10:00 am, visit the tea-house near West Market to s

thoughts.subject: self
thoughts.description: He feels calmer when he copies a difficult passage until the brushwork settles his thoughts.

thoughts.subject: self
thoughts.description: He promised himself he would not return home without giving the examinations his best effort.

thoughts.subject: self
thoughts.description: Festival distractions make concentration harder, but he also knows social encounters can yield useful advice.

thoughts.subject: self
thoughts.description: He is curious whether foreign merchants hear rumors of court priorities before local scholars do.

thoughts.subject: self
thoughts.description: He intends to ask anyone well-informed whether current examiners value moral clarity over ornament.

thoughts.subject: self
thoughts.description: He envies candidates who seem naturally graceful with parallel prose.

thoughts.subject: self
thoughts.description: He once impressed a county magistrate by composing a neat regulated verse on short notice.

thoughts.subject: self
thoughts.description: He respects Li Mingzhu because her tea house often gathers clerks, scholars, and travelers with useful news.

thoughts.subject: self
thoughts.description: He has heard that the preferences of court examiners shift with factional politics.

thoughts.subject: self
thoughts.description: He worries that his prose is sound but not elegant enough for metropolitan examiners.

thoughts.subject: self
thoughts.description: He can still recite passages from the Analects that his village tutor drilled into him as a child.

thoughts.subject: self
thoughts.description: His father sold part of the family grain store to fund this journey to Chang'an.

Excuse me, may I ask who you are? I don't recall seeing you in this dormitory before. I am Chen Zian, a scholar preparing for the imperial examinations. Pardon my surprise - I was asleep when I noticed you here.
An Lushan
